howItWorks.intro: This salary calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How is hourly rate converted to annual?
faq.answer: Hourly × hours per week × 52 weeks. A $30/hour rate at 40 hours/week = $62,400 annually.

faq.question: Does this include overtime?
faq.answer: This assumes straight-time hours only. Overtime (1.5x for hours over 40/week) would increase actual earnings.

faq.question: What about paid time off?
faq.answer: This calculation assumes 52 working weeks. If you get 2 weeks PTO, you still earn the same annual amount — just working 50 weeks instead of 52.

faq.question: How do I compare salaries across cities?
faq.answer: Use a cost-of-living calculator to adjust. A $65K salary in Kansas City may provide the same lifestyle as $95K in San Francisco.

faq.question: Is the Salary Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Salary Calculator?
sections.body: The Salary Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your amount, period, hours per week, you get instant results including hourly, weekly, monthly. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting hourly right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your amount and need to find the right hourly. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
